mechanism_narrative: A downward trend in FEDFUNDS lowers discount rates and risk premia, so S&P 500 forward returns are positive over the next month when the policy rate is falling from already-low levels.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of periods with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or the rolling 2-year rank correlation stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income and rising recession risk, which should depress equity prices and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes generate positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average forward return is not materially below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates after a prior soft patch, it indicates improving nominal demand and revenue expectations, which should lift S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change turns positive after being negative in the prior 3-month window
failure_condition: Reject if positive reversals in RSAFS do not precede above-median 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal flips sign in more than half of rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in PAYEMS and decline in UNRATE confirms labor-market broadening, which supports consumer income and earnings expectations and should yield positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition PAYEMS rising and UNRATE falling does not outperform single-variable labor signals, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of qualified instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and capacity utilization, improving cyclically sensitive earnings and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change is positive for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum does not lead to above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal loses sign consistency across successive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls sharply while RSAFS remains weak, the economy is losing housing-led demand support, and equities should underperform over the next month because earnings breadth deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 5% over 3 months and RSAFS 3-month growth is below zero
failure_condition: Reject if the combined housing-demand weakness condition fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening regime, a high or rising FEDFUNDS rate raises financing costs and compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 25 bps over 2 meetings
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not negative after tightening episodes, or if the predictive effect vanishes once FEDFUNDS is above prior-cycle peaks.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth and payroll growth both reaccelerate together, it signals synchronized real-demand improvement that should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; the effect fails if GDP improves without labor-market confirmation.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and PAYEMS both show positive 3-month momentum
failure_condition: Reject if synchronized growth in GDPC1 and PAYEMS does not produce positive 60-day SPX returns in a clear majority of cases, or if the effect is present when only one of the two series improves.
